channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: James Burrows, director of classic shows 'Cheers' and 'Friends,' dies at 85
item.description: Burrows spent his career behind the camera specializing in situation comedies. Few viewers recognized him or knew his name, other than to see it flash quickly on the screen in the opening credits. But they knew his work.
item.content:encoded:
Burrows spent his career behind the camera specializing in situation comedies. Few viewers recognized him or knew his name, other than to see it flash quickly on the screen in the opening credits. But they knew his work.

(Image credit: Jason LaVeris)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Air Force One, gifted to Trump from Qatar, arrives at Joint Base Andrews
item.description: The luxury Boeing 747, initially valued at $400 million, arrived ahead of schedule on Friday. The jet caused controversy as one of the biggest foreign gifts ever received by the U.S. government.
item.content:encoded:
The luxury Boeing 747, initially valued at $400 million, arrived ahead of schedule on Friday. The jet caused controversy as one of the biggest foreign gifts ever received by the U.S. government.

(Image credit: Alex Wong
)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Italy's Meloni, once Trump's closest ally in Europe, says he made up a story about her
item.description: "Italy and I do not beg," Meloni said in a video rebuke posted on social media Friday. Italy's top diplomat, meanwhile, said he was cancelling a visit to the U.S because of the alleged remarks.
item.content:encoded:
"Italy and I do not beg," Meloni said in a video rebuke posted on social media Friday. Italy's top diplomat, meanwhile, said he was cancelling a visit to the U.S because of the alleged remarks.

(Image credit: Geert Vanden Wijngaert)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Rosman

item.title: Algae clouded Trump's vision for the Reflecting Pool. But scientists aren't surprised
item.description: The shallow, sunny waters of the reflecting pool are an ideal incubator for algae growth in the summertime. Experts say the recent renovation may have helped accelerate it.
item.content:encoded:
The shallow, sunny waters of the reflecting pool are an ideal incubator for algae growth in the summertime. Experts say the recent renovation may have helped accelerate it.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: US-Iran talks in Switzerland canceled. And, DHS to give police facial recognition app
item.description: The U.S.-Iran talks that were set to happen in Switzerland have been canceled. And, the Department of Homeland Security has plans to give some local police access to ICE facial recognition technology.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S.-Iran talks that were set to happen in Switzerland have been canceled. And, the Department of Homeland Security has plans to give some local police access to ICE facial recognition technology.

(Image credit: Ken Cedeno)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: What you need to know about the preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement signed by Trump
item.description: Here's a look at the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the challenges that remain to find lasting peace.
item.content:encoded:
Here's a look at the preliminary agreement between the U.S. and Iran, and the challenges that remain to find lasting peace.

(Image credit: Iranian Presidency Office via AP)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Some local police have access to an ICE facial recognition app
item.description: A document from the Department of Homeland Security outlines plans to issue local police facial recognition technology used by federal immigration agents, a move that will expand the scope of ICE surveillance.
item.content:encoded:
A document from the Department of Homeland Security outlines plans to issue local police facial recognition technology used by federal immigration agents, a move that will expand the scope of ICE surveillance.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: It's toys vs. tech in 'Toy Story 5.' Here are 4 ways to keep tech in check this summer
item.description: Kids' screen use goes way up in the summertime. And just as the movie Toy Story 5 portrays, that can be problematic for children. Here are tips for parents to help their kids manage screens and have fun IRL this summer.
item.content:encoded:
Toy Story 5. It opens in theaters on Friday, June 19.'/>

Kids' screen use goes way up in the summertime. And just as the movie Toy Story 5 portrays, that can be problematic for children. Here are tips for parents to help their kids manage screens and have fun IRL this summer.

(Image credit: Disney/Pixar)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: Why did the newly refurbished Reflecting Pool turn green? The quiz knows
item.description: Plus, keep an eye out for our World Cup pun, intrigue around a tarp, and the Obama Presidential Center.
item.content:encoded: Plus, keep an eye out for our World Cup pun, intrigue around a tarp, and the Obama Presidential Center.
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Big dogs, Buc-ee's and the great BBQ debate: World Cup fans discover everyday America
item.description: After a week of the World Cup, visitors to the U.S. are marveling on social media about things like free drink refills. It's a respite as tensions between Washington and its allies run high.
item.content:encoded: After a week of the World Cup, visitors to the U.S. are marveling on social media about things like free drink refills. It's a respite as tensions between Washington and its allies run high.
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger
